title: Elementary process plant occupations n.e.c.
description: Perform a variety of attending duties at amusement or recreation facility. May schedule use of recreation facilities, maintain and provide equipment to participants of sporting events or recreational pursuits, or operate amusement concessions and rides. Directly supervise and coordinate activities of construction or extraction workers. Direct work crews installing residential or commercial solar photovoltaic or thermal systems. (Based on 74 related US occupations.)

Operating Vehicles, Mechanized Devices, or Equipment

Performing for or Working Directly with the Public

tasks.task: Add chemicals, such as ammonia, chlorine, or lime, to disinfect and deodorize water and other liquids.
Signal workers to start geological material extraction or boring.
Unload materials, devices, and machine parts, using hand tools.
Dismantle extracting and boring equipment used for excavation with hand tools and provide assistance to extraction craft workers.
Organize materials to prepare for use, clean up work areas, and remove debris after activities are complete.
Drive moving equipment to transport materials and parts to excavation sites and observe equipment operation to detect problems.
Repair and maintain automotive and drilling equipment, using hand tools, and inspect trucks prior to beginning routes.
Fill out defective equipment reports and communicate with dispatchers concerning delays or equipment breakdowns.
Drive trucks following established routes through various areas, ensuring safe operating conditions and checking road or weather effects.
Dump refuse or recyclable materials at disposal sites and clean trucks or compactor bodies after routes have been completed.
Operate and adjust controls on equipment to purify and clarify water, process or dispose of sewage, and generate power.
Collect and test water and sewage samples using test equipment and clean and maintain tanks and work areas.
Direct and coordinate plant workers engaged in routine operations and maintenance activities, recording operational data as needed.
Signal equipment operators for alignment or adjustment of machinery and read plans or specifications to determine work activities.
Clean or prepare construction sites to eliminate hazards, and confirm safety of operation with accurate timekeeping and inspections.
Collect and deliver sample solutions for laboratory analysis and test materials using testing equipment.
Erect scaffolding or other installation structures and clean work areas and equipment to maintain a safe job site.
Provide assistance to craft workers and follow instructions to locate and supply materials for installation tasks.
Remove damaged materials and prepare surfaces using hand tools and repair techniques to ensure quality maintenance.
Operate or maintain equipment and tools that facilitate various construction or repair processes, while ensuring efficiency and safety.
Measure, cut, thread and assemble new pipe, placing the assembled pipe in hangers or other supports.
Install copper-clad ground rods, using a manual post driver, and mount brackets and hangers on walls to hold pipes.
Fit or assist in fitting valves, couplings, or assemblies to tanks, pumps, or systems, using hand tools.
Excavate and grade ditches, and lay and join pipe for water and sewer service, including cutting and lifting pipe.
Clean shop, work area, and machines, and maintain tools, vehicles, and equipment and keep parts and supplies in order.
Raise, lower, or position equipment, tools, and materials, and erect electrical system components, rig scaffolds, hoists, and shoring.
Document and prepare reports of accidents, unscheduled stops, or delays, and maintain processing and production information.
Operate various machines and tools to perform electrical installations, repairs, and maintenance of systems and equipment.
Perform safety checks on equipment before operating, including conducting methane gas checks to ensure air quality.
Install, maintain, and repair roof systems, including applying materials like shingles, and performing emergency repairs and general maintenance.
Operate and drive locomotives, diesel switch engines, flatcars, and railcars in train yards and industrial sites.
Signal other workers for setting brakes and throwing track switches during car switching activities.
Monitor gauges, recording instruments, and products to ensure specified operating conditions are maintained.
Inspect tracks, cars, and engines for defects and determine service needs by sending them for repair if necessary.
Perform routine maintenance and make minor repairs on machines and equipment, such as replacing parts and lubricating.
Record operational data like temperatures, pressures, ingredients used, and processing times on report forms.
Observe safety precautions while performing tasks to prevent fires or explosions in hazardous areas.
Monitor pressure gauges and adjust controls to maintain desired conditions such as flow rate and temperature.
Clean and sterilize tanks and equipment using hoses, brushes, or chemical solutions to maintain safe operations.
Communicate processing instructions clearly to other workers to ensure coordination of operations.
Examine roadway and clear obstructions from the path of travel.
Examine products to verify conformance to quality standards and inspect materials and products for defects and specified requirements.
Operate machinery used in the production process and assist machine operators while monitoring machine performance to detect problems.
Remove products, waste material, or attachments from machines and load or unload items from machines, conveyors, and conveyances.
Clean and maintain machinery, equipment, and work areas to ensure proper functioning and safe working conditions.
Record production and operational data, such as the amount of materials processed, and maintain logs of recycling materials received or shipped.
Transfer raw materials, finished products, and tools between storage and work areas using hand trucks or powered lift trucks.
Perform routine maintenance on machinery and equipment, including troubleshooting and minor repairs as necessary.
Adjust, repair, or replace components of machines and equipment, and assemble or install equipment using hand and power tools.
Test and replace electrical or mechanical components, and calibrate devices such as liquid flow meters and machinery controls.
Collaborate with others to repair or move machines, and provide training on equipment operation and safety procedures.
Read and understand operating manuals, blueprints, and technical drawings.
Examine and inspect parts for defects, such as breakage or excessive wear, using relevant measuring tools.
Repair or replace defective parts using hand tools, machining tools, power tools, or welding equipment.
Clean, lubricate, and perform routine maintenance on equipment and vehicles.
Overhaul and test machines or equipment to verify repairs and ensure operating efficiency.
Supervise and coordinate work activities of construction or extraction workers, ensuring compliance with safety standards.
Estimate materials, equipment, and personnel needed for solar installation projects based on specifications.
Plan and coordinate installations of photovoltaic (PV) solar and thermal systems to meet codes and requirements.
Assess solar installation sites and customer needs to determine feasibility and design requirements.
Provide technical assistance and supervision to solar installers and technicians on solar systems installations.
Monitor work of contractors and inspection progress on construction sites to ensure specifications are met.
Collaborate with workers to resolve production problems and improve methods for solar installation projects.
Train workers in construction methods, operation of equipment, safety procedures, or company policies.
Assign work to employees based on material or worker requirements for specific jobs, and monitor activities to ensure adherence to rules and safety procedures.
Order or requisition materials or supplies, and maintain supplies of tools, equipment, and materials, ordering additional supplies as needed.
Record information on specified forms or reports, including details of attendance, sales, receipts, or repair activities.
Locate, measure, and mark site locations or placement of structures or equipment using measuring and marking equipment, and verify accuracy using calipers and templates.
Verify, collect, or punch tickets before admitting patrons to venues, and provide information about facilities, entertainment options, and rules and regulations.
Sell tickets and collect fees from customers, and rent, sell, or issue sporting equipment and supplies.
Direct patrons to rides, seats, or attractions, and assist patrons entering or exiting amusement rides or mounting or dismounting animals.
Keep informed of shut-down and emergency evacuation procedures, and perform minor repairs, adjustments, or maintenance tasks on equipment.
Clean sporting equipment, vehicles, rides, booths, facilities, or grounds, and maintain inventories of equipment, storing and retrieving items as necessary.
Start operation of rolling and milling machines to flatten, temper, form, and reduce sheet metal sections and to produce steel strips.
Manipulate controls and observe dial indicators to monitor, adjust, and regulate speeds of machine mechanisms and machine cycles to detect jamming.
Read rolling orders, blueprints, and mill schedules to determine setup specifications, work sequences, product dimensions, and installation procedures.
Examine and inspect raw materials and finished products to verify conformance to specifications.
Adjust and correct machine set-ups to reduce thicknesses, reshape products, and eliminate product defects, optimizing machine performance.
Sort recyclable materials into appropriate containers, including metals, glass, wood, paper, and plastics.
Operate forklifts and other equipment to load heavy items onto trucks for shipping.
Clean machines, inspect and lubricate equipment, and perform routine maintenance or minor repairs.
Monitor employees' work levels, evaluate performance, and conduct training in equipment operations and safety procedures.
Confer with management and other supervisors to coordinate operations, resolve worker problems, and implement safety regulations.
